# Free Email Signature Generator — Full Documentation

> A free, open-source HTML email signature generator at https://signatures.clarkemedia.ie. No sign-up, no tracking, no subscriptions. Runs entirely in the browser. MIT licensed.

## Overview

This is a browser-based tool for creating professional HTML email signatures. Users fill in a form with their details, customise the appearance, and copy the generated signature into their email client. The entire tool is a single HTML file with no server-side component, no database, no cookies, and no analytics. Nothing leaves the browser.

## URL

https://signatures.clarkemedia.ie

## Source Code

https://github.com/clarkemedia/email-signature-generator

## Creator

Eoin McMahon, Founder of Clarke Media (https://clarkemedia.ie)
Based in Ireland.

## License

MIT — free for personal and commercial use with no restrictions.

## Supported Email Clients

The generated signatures work with:
- Microsoft Outlook (new version, Windows and web)
- Microsoft Outlook Classic (desktop, Windows)
- Microsoft Outlook for Mac
- Gmail (web)
- Apple Mail (macOS)
- Any email client that supports HTML signatures

The tool includes step-by-step setup instructions for each of these clients.

## Customizable Fields

All fields are optional. Leaving a field blank automatically hides it from the generated signature.

### Personal Details
- Full name
- Job title
- Company name

### Contact Information
- Phone number (with clickable tel: link)
- Email address (with clickable mailto: link)
- Website URL (with clickable https: link)

### Branding
- Company logo (via URL or local file upload)
- Accent colour (hex colour picker)
- Font family (web-safe fonts: Arial, Verdana, Tahoma, Trebuchet MS, Georgia, Times New Roman, Courier New)

### Social Media Links
- LinkedIn
- Twitter / X
- Facebook
- Instagram
- GitHub

Social icons only appear when a URL is provided. Icons are sourced from Icons8.

### Disclaimer
- Toggle on/off
- Fully editable text
- Auto-bolds the first word before a colon (e.g. "Important:" becomes bold)

## Output Format

The generated signature uses:
- Table-based HTML layout for maximum email client compatibility
- Inline CSS styles (no external stylesheets)
- Web-safe fonts
- Proper cellpadding, cellspacing, and border attributes
- Responsive design considerations
- Properly escaped HTML entities for security

## Copy Options

1. Copy signature — copies as rich text (text/html MIME type) for pasting directly into email client signature settings
2. Copy HTML source — copies the raw HTML for manual insertion into signature files

## Preview Modes

- Light mode — white/light background preview
- Dark mode — dark background preview to check visibility of logos and text

## Technical Details

- Single HTML file, no build step, no frameworks, no dependencies
- Client-side only — no server communication
- XSS prevention via HTML entity escaping
- FileReader API for local logo upload (base64 data URL)
- ClipboardItem API for rich text copy with fallback to execCommand
- Google Fonts (Rubik) for the UI only — signatures use web-safe fonts

## Comparison with Paid Alternatives

| Feature | This Tool | Typical Paid Tools |
|---|---|---|
| Price | Free | $3-10/month per user |
| Sign-up required | No | Yes |
| Tracking/analytics | None | Yes |
| Branding added to signature | No | Often (on free tiers) |
| Open source | Yes (MIT) | No |
| Data sent to server | Nothing | Your details |
| Works offline | Yes | No |
| Self-hostable | Yes | No |
| Feature limits | None | Common on free tiers |

## Common Use Cases

- Small business owners creating professional email signatures
- Freelancers and consultants setting up branded signatures
- Teams who need consistent email signatures without paying per-user fees
- Anyone who wants a quick, no-hassle email signature without creating an account
- Developers who want to self-host or customise the tool
- IT administrators setting up signatures for staff

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Is this really free?
Yes. No trial period, no feature gates, no hidden costs. The full tool is available at no charge under the MIT license.

### Do I need to create an account?
No. There is no sign-up, no login, no account creation. Just open the page and start using it.

### Is my data safe?
Yes. Everything runs in your browser. No data is sent to any server. There are no cookies, no analytics, no tracking scripts.

### Can I add my company logo?
Yes. You can paste a logo URL or upload an image file from your computer. For best results with dark mode email clients, use a JPEG with a solid background rather than a transparent PNG.

### What fonts are available?
The tool offers web-safe fonts that render reliably across all email clients: Arial, Verdana, Tahoma, Trebuchet MS, Georgia, Times New Roman, and Courier New.

### Does it work with Outlook?
Yes. It works with new Outlook (Windows/web), classic Outlook (desktop), and Outlook for Mac. Setup instructions are included for each version.

### Does it work with Gmail?
Yes. Copy the signature and paste it into Gmail's signature settings. Step-by-step instructions are included.

### Can I self-host this?
Yes. It is a single HTML file. Download it and serve it from any web server, or just open it locally in a browser.

### Can I use this for my whole team?
Yes. There are no per-user limits. Each person can generate their own signature independently.

### What social media icons are supported?
LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Facebook, Instagram, and GitHub. Icons only appear in the signature when you provide a URL.
